content.full_text:
LAW OFFICES OF

NIELSEN, MERKSAMER,
HODGSON, PARRINELLO & MUELLER
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

MARIN COUNTY
591 REDWOOD HIGHWAY, #4000
MILL VALLEY, CALIFORNIA 94941
TELEPHONE (415) 389-6800

770 L STREET, SUITE 800
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
TELEPHONE (916) 446-6752

SAN FRANCISCO
650 CALIFORNIA STREET, SUITE 2650
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94108
TELEPHONE (415) 989-6800

March 29, 1990

Mr. Scott Hallabrin
Legal Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, 8th Floor
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Request For Advice/Confirmation Of Advice

Dear Scott:

This is to confirm your recent telephone advice to me
concerning application of the "membership communications"
exemption from the provisions of the Govt. Code sections 85301-
85303, as provided in the preliminary injunction in Service
Employees International Union, et al. v. Fair Political Practices
Commission (Case No. CIVS-89-043 LKK-JFM). I understand that
such advice would be valid as long as the injunction remains in
effect.

1. CONFIRMATION OF TELEPHONE ADVICE

I asked several questions concerning the scope of this
"member communication" exemption, and you rendered the following
advice:

A. A sponsored political committee or broad-based
political committee may pay for communications made to
members of its sponsoring organization coordinated with
a candidate, and the expenditures for such
communications are exempt from the contribution limits
of Govt. Code section 85303.

B. A member of the organization is subject to the
contribution limits of Govt. Code section 85301 if he
or she makes expenditures for communications to other
members of the membership organization that endorse a
candidate, when made at the candidate's behest. The
limits apply even if the member's intention is to rebut
the candidate endorsement made by the membership
organization which is not subject to the section 85301
limits.

Mr. Scott Hallabrin
March 29, 1990
Page 2


     C.  A membership organization or its sponsored
committee will be exempt from the limits of Govt. Code
sections 85301 or 85303 if as part of its membership
endorsement communication to its membership, it urges
members to contribute to a candidate and to facilitate
making the contribution, provides the members with a
return envelope with the organization's return address
paid for by the organization.  The same result would
obtain if the membership organization sends a member
communication to its members which includes a return
envelope with the candidate's return address paid for
by the candidate.  However, you concluded that if the
organization or its sponsored committee pays for an
envelope with the candidate's return address on it,
that will be a "contribution" subject to the limits of
Govt. Code sections 85301 or 85303.

2.  REQUEST FOR FURTHER ADVICE

     Assume that a membership association or its sponsored
committee publishes a communication to its members which endorses
a candidate and also invites members to attend a fundraising
event sponsored by the organization on behalf of the candidate.
Are the expenditures for the mailing to the membership
"contributions" subject to the limits of Govt. Code sections
85301 or 85303 or exempt under the "member communications"
exemption?

     Thank you very much for your response.  Please contact
me if you have any questions regarding this request or if I have
misstated your responses.

                              Very truly yours,

                              [signature]
                              CHARLES H. BELL, JR.

CHB:deh
6205.02
